title: Xanthophyllum excelsum
taxonomy: Xanthophyllum excelsum belongs to the family Polygalaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the milkwort family. Xanthophyllum excelsum is a species within this family and is native to tropical Africa. The Polygalaceae family is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Xanthophyllum excelsum is also classified as a member of the order Polygalales.
uses: Xanthophyllum excelsum is reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In some cultures, it is also used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits and potential side effects. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and terpenoids.
